subject: Use released version 1.5.1 of CCSDK parent poms
commit_message:
Use released version 1.5.1 of CCSDK parent poms

Use released version 1.5.1 of CCSDK parent poms

Change-Id: I3644ef502540210e873a3f93ab4b1990c0bd92af
Issue-ID: CCSDK-1963
Signed-off-by: Dan Timoney <dtimoney@att.com>

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> (1 comment)

This change was needed to resolve build issue - compile was failing because version 0.7.0 (value of this property in released version l.5.1 parent pom) does not yet exist.  Also, no project outside this repo should use this parent pom - external projects should use ccsdk/parent poms

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > (1 comment)
> 
> This change was needed to resolve build issue - compile was failing because version 0.7.0 (value of this property in released version l.5.1 parent pom) does not yet exist.  Also, no project outside this repo should use this parent pom - external projects should use ccsdk/parent poms

In that case, can we have range instead a hardocded version for property defined in ccsdk/parent poms.

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=ccsdk/parent.git;a=blob;f=springboot/springboot2/pom.xml;h=b688874da9528677ddca2f0f19244f6314ad80ae;hb=refs/heads/master#l87

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> > > 
> > > (1 comment)
> > 
> > This change was needed to resolve build issue - compile was failing because version 0.7.0 (value of this property in released version l.5.1 parent pom) does not yet exist.  Also, no project outside this repo should use this parent pom - external projects should use ccsdk/parent poms
> 
> In that case, can we have range instead a hardocded version for property defined in ccsdk/parent poms.
> 
> https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=ccsdk/parent.git;a=blob;f=springboot/springboot2/pom.xml;h=b688874da9528677ddca2f0f19244f6314ad80ae;hb=refs/heads/master#l87

Ranges are problematic, since their behavior is not consistent.  Sometimes ranges are resolved at compile time (e.g. when single-feature-parent generates feature.xml file), which means that features need to be recompiled and redeployed if their dependencies are updated. Treatment of snapshot versions within ranges is different between maven and Karaf ... for example, Karaf considers 0.7.0-SNAPSHOT to be in the range [0.7.0, 0.8.0) but maven does not.

For that reason, we do not currently use ranges in any of our ONAP parent poms and we are trying to find ways to avoid their use in downstreams as well.

More generally, all CCSDK repos have been updated to ensure that none of them use the property values specified in the ccsdk/parent poms for their own CCSDK dependencies, so that CCSDK can test SNAPSHOT releases across its repos without exposing clients (like APPC or SDNC) to snapshots.  This change is consistent with that approach (i.e. CDS should override the cds version property in the ccsdk/parent poms for its own internal dependencies).

> > 
> > https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=ccsdk/parent.git;a=blob;f=springboot/springboot2/pom.xml;h=b688874da9528677ddca2f0f19244f6314ad80ae;hb=refs/heads/master#l87
> 
> Ranges are problematic, since their behavior is not consistent.  Sometimes ranges are resolved at compile time (e.g. when single-feature-parent generates feature.xml file), which means that features need to be recompiled and redeployed if their dependencies are updated. Treatment of snapshot versions within ranges is different between maven and Karaf ... for example, Karaf considers 0.7.0-SNAPSHOT to be in the range [0.7.0, 0.8.0) but maven does not.
> 
> For that reason, we do not currently use ranges in any of our ONAP parent poms and we are trying to find ways to avoid their use in downstreams as well.
> 
> More generally, all CCSDK repos have been updated to ensure that none of them use the property values specified in the ccsdk/parent poms for their own CCSDK dependencies, so that CCSDK can test SNAPSHOT releases across its repos without exposing clients (like APPC or SDNC) to snapshots.  This change is consistent with that approach (i.e. CDS should override the cds version property in the ccsdk/parent poms for its own internal dependencies).

I agree with you:

In Maven:   X.X.X-SANPSHOT < X.X.X
In OSGI:    X.X.X-SANPSHOT > X.X.X

Maven range is all about dependencies on Module Compile Time
OSGI Range is all about dependencies on Feature Install/Run Time

So there we had a workaround to use version as (0.6.999, 0.7.999)
Which works for both Maven as well as OSGI, and in real time we never reach to a artifact version with extension of 999.

Right now, this review request can be merged, but if this module to be referred in some other module then other module must to be on same version else it will not build the child module.

Agreed.  Can you please +2 so I can merge?  Thanks!
